name: Thrust-to-Weight Ratio Calculator
description: Compute the thrust-to-weight ratio for rockets and aircraft.
formulaDescription: Thrust-to-weight ratio compares engine thrust to the weight of the vehicle. Uses g = 9.80665 m/s².

example.explanation: A 1,000,000 N thrust on a 100,000 kg vehicle gives a thrust-to-weight ratio of about 1.02.

faqs.question: What is a good thrust-to-weight ratio?
faqs.answer: A ratio above 1 means the vehicle can accelerate upward; rockets often have ratios above 1.2 at liftoff.
